source.definition: to come to a conclusion or finish; to reach the final point of something

explanation.sourceText: The English word "end" originates from Old English "endian", meaning to bring to a close, related to the root "end" which signifies the final point or limit.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „end“ provine din engleza veche „endian“, care înseamnă a aduce la încheiere, legat de rădăcina „end“ ce semnifică punctul final sau limita.

target.definition: a ajunge la sfârșit; a încheia

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a termina“ derives from the Latin „terminare“, meaning to finish. It refers to setting a final point in a process.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a termina“ derivă din latinescul „terminare“, care înseamnă „a încheia“, indicând stabilirea unui punct final într-un proces.

target.additional: Regular first conjugation verb (-a). Used in many contexts, from everyday tasks to complex projects.

examples.sourceText: I end my work at five o'clock.
We end the meeting at one.
I have successfully ended my study.
examples.targetText: Am terminat cu succes studiul meu.

examples.sourceText: I would have to end it if I had time.
examples.targetText: Ar trebui să îl termin, dacă aș avea timp.

learningTips.text: Remember that the Romanian verb "a termina" sounds like the English word "terminate", which also means to end or finish.
Think of the English word "terminal" (like an airport terminal) as a place where journeys end, helping you recall "termina" means to finish.
Practice with simple sentences like "Eu termin" (I end) to get comfortable using the verb in daily conversations.
The English root "term" relates to limits or ends, just like "a termina" in Romanian, so linking these can help you remember the meaning.
